political.s: Toyota Motor North America contributed $1.15 million to federal candidates in 2023-2024. The company briefly paused PAC donations to Republicans who objected to the 2020 election certification but resumed donations after just three weeks, supporting election objectors despite earlier concerns.
political.amt: $1.15-1.74 million (PAC and lobbying combined estimated at $7.9 million annually)

charity.s: Toyota USA Foundation is a $100 million endowment supporting STEM education and workforce development. The company also operates Toyota4Good corporate giving program and Mobility Foundation supporting transportation and community initiatives in operational areas.
charity.focus: STEM education, workforce readiness, community development

environment.s: Toyota has documented systematic Clean Air Act violations (2005-2015), paid $180 million EPA penalty for delayed emissions defect reporting. Current emissions targets lag EPA 2032 standards, with hybrid-heavy strategy at odds with rapid EV transition requirements. Subsidiary Hino Motors faced $1.6 billion settlement for emissions fraud.

labor.s: Toyota has $9.1 billion in tracked penalties since 2000. Labor violations include NLRB finding against California dealership for refusing to rehire strike participants. Limited public OSHA data available but documented violations at manufacturing facilities.

dei.s: Toyota withdrew from HRC Corporate Equality Index and suspended external DEI initiatives in October 2024 following conservative activist pressure, citing focus on STEM and workforce readiness. Company continues internal Business Partnering Groups but ended participation in Pride events and cultural festivals.

guns.s: Not applicable to automotive manufacturer beyond general vehicle sales

privacy.s: Toyota collects extensive connected vehicle data including precise geolocation, driving behavior, and biometric information with vague consent language. Mozilla Foundation rates practices poorly; lawsuit claims data shared with insurance companies without clear consumer understanding. Multiple complex privacy policies across platforms.

execPay.s: Toyota Chairman Akio Toyoda earned record $10.09 million (¥1.622 billion) in 2024. CEO Koji Sato received $5.67 million. High executive pay despite scrutiny, though chairman compensation increased while company profits declined.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
